name: Lab Interpretation — Mixed Acid-Base Disorder
description: Level 4 — handcrafted — mixed respiratory alkalosis + metabolic acidosis in sepsis with compensation analysis
prompt:
Interpret the following laboratory results for a 58-year-old male admitted with fever, hypotension, and altered mental status (suspected sepsis):

Arterial Blood Gas:
- pH: 7.38 (reference: 7.35–7.45) — apparently normal
- pCO2: 24 mmHg (reference: 35–45 mmHg)
- pO2: 68 mmHg (reference: 85–105 mmHg) on FiO2 0.40
- HCO3 (calculated): 14 mEq/L (reference: 22–26 mEq/L)
- Base Excess: -11 mEq/L (reference: -2 to +2)

Electrolytes:
- Sodium: 142 mEq/L (reference: 133–145) — normal
- Chloride: 104 mEq/L (reference: 96–108) — normal
- Bicarbonate: 14 mEq/L (reference: 22–32)
- Potassium: 4.1 mEq/L — normal
- Anion Gap: 24 mEq/L (reference: 8–20) [Na - (Cl + HCO3) = 142 - (104+14) = 24]

Other:
- Lactate: 6.8 mmol/L (reference: 0.5–2.0 mmol/L)
- Glucose: 188 mg/dL (reference: 70–100)
- Creatinine: 2.2 mg/dL (reference: 0.5–1.2)
- WBC: 24,000 /uL with left shift

Provide stepwise acid-base interpretation, identify all disorders present, and assess whether pH is appropriately explained.

abnormal_values.significance: pH appears normal but CONCEALS severe mixed acid-base disorder — must analyze components
Severe hypocapnia — primary respiratory alkalosis or compensation for metabolic acidosis
Severely low bicarbonate — primary metabolic acidosis
High anion gap metabolic acidosis — unmeasured anions present (lactic acid)
Severely elevated lactate — primary cause of high anion gap; indicates severe tissue hypoperfusion
Hypoxemia despite 40% FiO2 — P/F ratio 68/0.4 = 170, consistent with ARDS or severe pneumonia
Leukocytosis with left shift — severe bacterial infection / sepsis

expected.interpretation: MIXED ACID-BASE DISORDER: Primary high anion gap metabolic acidosis (HCO3 14, AG 24) from severe lactic acidosis (lactate 6.8) AND concurrent primary respiratory alkalosis (pCO2 24) from sepsis-driven hyperventilation. The two disorders mask each other, producing a near-normal pH. Expected pCO2 for metabolic compensation = 1.5×14+8 = 29 mmHg; actual pCO2 of 24 is LOWER than expected → additional primary respiratory alkalosis confirmed. Underlying cause: septic shock with lactic acidosis + early ARDS.
expected.pattern_recognition: Key to unmask: pH 7.38 (normal) + HCO3 14 (very low) + pCO2 24 (very low) = MIXED disorder. Metabolic acidosis from lactic acidosis (AG 24, lactate 6.8) + respiratory alkalosis from sepsis hyperventilation that EXCEEDS expected compensation. P/F ratio 170 suggests concurrent ARDS. Delta AG / Delta HCO3 = (24-12)/(24-14) = 1.2 — suggests pure high AG metabolic acidosis without superimposed metabolic alkalosis.
differential_considerations: High anion gap metabolic acidosis from lactic acidosis type A (tissue hypoperfusion in septic shock)
Primary respiratory alkalosis from sepsis-driven hyperventilation and/or early ARDS
Possible early acute kidney injury (Cr 2.2) contributing to metabolic acidosis
ARDS from sepsis (P/F ratio 170 on FiO2 40%)
